jokes.text: ALL THE THINGS MAKE ME FEEL A LOT OF FEELINGS ABOUT MYSELF.
jokes.setup: Leslie's discussing focus group feedback about her campaign
jokes.punchline: Her awkward, overly vulnerable admission about having 'feelings about herself'
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie over-sharing in campaign context. Good character work but predictable structure.

jokes.text: SHE'S KINDA SHORT, DON'T YOU THINK? AGGRESSIVELY SHORT ALMOST. IT'S LIKE SHE'S THROWING IT IN MY FACE.
jokes.setup: Focus group member critiquing Leslie's candidacy
jokes.punchline: 'Aggressively short' - treating height as an intentional hostile act
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation - height becomes aggressive behavior. High quotability and originality.

jokes.text: INSIGHTFUL.
jokes.punchline: Dry, sarcastic one-word dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response. Great economy but not particularly original.

jokes.text: SHE DOESN'T SEEM LIKE THE KIND OF PERSON YOU COULD GO BOWLING WITH
jokes.setup: Focus group member explaining why he won't vote for Leslie
jokes.punchline: Using 'bowling compatibility' as a political metric
jokes.explanation: Sets up the entire episode's premise. Solid observational humor about political accessibility.

jokes.text: WHAT? THAT'S RIDICULOUS. I AM EXCELLENT AT BOWLING. ASK RON.
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate defensive overreaction and oddly specific Ron reference
jokes.explanation: Establishes the 'Ask Ron' running gag. Perfect Leslie character moment of missing the point.

jokes.text: I'M JUST REMINDING MYSELF TO TELL RON TO TELL YOU HOW GOOD I AM AT BOWLING.
jokes.setup: Ben telling Leslie not to obsess over one comment
jokes.punchline: Leslie's absurd chain-of-command plan to prove her bowling skills
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the Ron reference. Shows Leslie's inability to let things go through absurd logic.

jokes.text: I ONCE KNEW A LESLIE, AND, UH, SHE WAS JUST AWFUL. SO NOW I HATE ALL LESLIES. WOULD SHE CONSIDER CHANGING HER NAME?
jokes.setup: Focus group member saying he'd vote for Leslie
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining it with irrational name-based prejudice and ridiculous solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and absurd logic. Great structure with the positive setup immediately undermined.

jokes.text: I CAN'T TELL YOU HOW MANY TIMES A FUN TUG-OF-WAR WITH A DOG OVER A CHEW TOY TURNED CONTENTIOUS. ONE OF US ALWAYS ENDS UP MAD.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining she's not good at letting things go
jokes.punchline: Treating dog toy disputes as serious relationship conflicts
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie overly-serious approach to trivial matters. Good character integration.

jokes.text: I DID SOME RESEARCH TOO ABOUT THE GUY WHO SAID THE THING ABOUT ME AND BOWLING, AND HIS NAME IS DEREK.
jokes.punchline: The 'research' is just learning his name
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's obsessive tendencies and misplaced priorities. Good character work.

jokes.text: AND GET THIS--HE'S NOT EVEN THAT GOOD OF A BOWLER. HIS AVERAGE IS 132, ACCORDING TO THE MOST RECENT AVAILABLE DATA.
jokes.punchline: She actually investigated his bowling statistics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Leslie's obsessive research abilities applied to petty concerns. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: OH, MY GOD.
jokes.setup: Ben's reaction to Leslie's bowling research
jokes.punchline: His horrified realization of how deep Leslie's obsession goes
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight man reaction. Economy is excellent but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: WOULD THEY DEIGN TO SAY SUCH THINGS TO WOODROW WILSON OR BENJAMIN DISRAELI-- OKAY, I SEE WHAT YOU'RE TALKING ABOUT.
jokes.setup: Leslie arguing the criticism is sexist
jokes.punchline: Mid-sentence realization that she's proving their point about being elitist
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware moment. Perfect structure with the immediate self-correction. Very Leslie-specific references.

jokes.text: NO, I DON'T THINK I BELIEVE YOU. IS THERE ANYONE I CAN ASK?
jokes.setup: Leslie claims she's good at bowling
jokes.punchline: Ben's mock-serious request for verification
jokes.explanation: Sets up the punchline perfectly. Good callback setup but not exceptional.

jokes.text: YEAH. ASK RON.
jokes.setup: Ben's sarcastic question about who to ask
jokes.punchline: Leslie completely missing the sarcasm and giving the expected answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the setup. Leslie's obliviousness to sarcasm is perfectly character-driven.

jokes.text: OH, I DON'T KNOW, JERRY. IT'S SUNDAY NIGHT, I'M MAKING PHONE CALLS TO STRANGERS, AND YOU'RE IN MY HOUSE. MY LIFE COULDN'T BE WORSE.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if everybody's feeling good about phone banking
jokes.punchline: April's brutal assessment of her current situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan delivery. The escalating list of mild annoyances as life's worst moments is very her.

jokes.text: LET'S GET THIS PHONE PARTY STARTED.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to energize the room
jokes.punchline: His painfully enthusiastic attempt at being cool
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry awkwardness. Predictable but character-appropriate cringe.

jokes.text: NO.
jokes.punchline: April's immediate, flat rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. Classic April shutdown.

jokes.text: NO ONE'S THINKING THAT.
jokes.setup: Ben asking how phone banking could be fun
jokes.punchline: Tom's blunt dismissal of the premise
jokes.explanation: Good economy and timing. Tom's directness cuts through Ben's forced enthusiasm.

jokes.text: I KINDA WAS.
jokes.setup: Tom saying no one was thinking that
jokes.punchline: Andy's innocent admission that he actually was
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy earnestness undermining Tom's cynicism. Good character contrast.

jokes.text: OH, MY GOD. I COULD USE THAT FOR A ROMANTIC NIGHT WITH MILLICENT GERGICH. OUT OF MY WAY, SUCKERS.
jokes.setup: Ben offering movie tickets as a prize
jokes.punchline: Chris's sudden intense enthusiasm for a modest prize
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chris's over-enthusiasm about simple pleasures.

jokes.text: AS CITY MANAGER, I PLAY NO FAVORITES. BUT AS A PRIVATE CITIZEN, I AM FREE TO SUPPORT WHOMEVER I CHOOSE, AND I CHOOSE TO SUPPORT TEAM KNOPE 'CAUSE THEY'RE THE BEST. EVERYBODY'S THE BEST. WE'RE ALL WINNERS.
jokes.setup: Chris trying to be impartial as city manager
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction followed by manic positivity spiral
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris character moment - his inability to maintain any position without excessive enthusiasm.

jokes.text: YOU LOOK LIKE YOU'RE TRYING TO LOOK LAID-BACK.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking if she looks laid-back
jokes.punchline: Ben's diplomatic way of saying she looks awkward
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the impossibility of forcing casualness.

jokes.text: THIS BOWLING ALLEY HAS MY FAVORITE RESTAURANT IN PAWNEE.
jokes.setup: Context of being at a bowling alley
jokes.punchline: Ron's deadpan declaration about bowling alley food
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron anti-pretension. His love of unpretentious food fits perfectly.

jokes.text: WHEN I EAT, IT IS THE FOOD THAT IS SCARED.
jokes.setup: Ben asking if Ron's scared to eat at the bowling alley
jokes.punchline: Ron's macho reversal of the fear dynamic
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron Swanson line. Perfect blend of absurdist logic and character-specific machismo. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: THAT IS LITERALLY THE MOST BEAUTIFUL NAME I HAVE EVER HEARD.
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top reaction to 'Deirdre Splatterfork'
jokes.explanation: Good Tom charm offensive moment. The absurd name adds to the humor.

jokes.text: I DON'T REALLY KNOW WHY YOU WOULD NEED MY SOCIAL SECURITY NUMBER, BUT--WELL, YEAH, NO. YOU'RE RIGHT. I GUESS IT COULDN'T HURT TO GIVE IT TO YOU. IT'S 210--
jokes.punchline: His willingness to give out personal information to obvious scammers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry gullibility moment. Painfully realistic character behavior.

jokes.text: [creepy voice] I'M CALLING FROM INSIDE YOUR HOUSE.
jokes.setup: April getting rejected for a donation
jokes.punchline: Her horror movie reference as fundraising tactic
jokes.explanation: Perfect April dark humor applied to mundane task. Very character-specific and creative.

jokes.text: THIS IS UNBELIEVABLE. I CANNOT BE STOPPED. * COME ON NOW GET SOME MONEY * * COME ON, APRIL GET AN EARPIECE * * COME ON DO IT *
jokes.punchline: His impromptu rap celebration
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom over-the-top celebration. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I DON'T CARE ABOUT THAT PRIZE, BUT I'M GONNA WIN BECAUSE I WANT HIS HAPPINESS TO GO AWAY.
jokes.punchline: Her purely spiteful reason for trying
jokes.explanation: Perfectly April motivation. Dark but honest, and the phrasing is very quotable.

jokes.text: TYPE IN 'T REX' FOR ME. NO, NO, NO. UM, 'T-BOZ.' WAIT, WAIT. 'TOMMY TSUNAMI.' NO, 'TICKY TICKY TOM-TOM.' NO, 'FLY GUY.'
jokes.setup: Tom trying to pick a bowling nickname
jokes.punchline: His increasingly ridiculous suggestions
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom trying-too-hard moment. Good character work but somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: I WROTE 'TOM.'
jokes.punchline: Donna's practical solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Tom's pretensions. Excellent economy and structure.

jokes.text: CLASSIC. TIMELESS. I LOVE IT.
jokes.punchline: Tom's face-saving overenthusiasm for the basic choice
jokes.explanation: Good character recovery moment. Tom's ability to spin anything positively.

jokes.text: OH, I WOULDN'T KNOW. I'M NEVER ON THE OTHER SIDE OF THE GLASS WITH THOSE THINGS.
jokes.setup: Derek recognizing Leslie from the focus group
jokes.punchline: Leslie's awkward attempt to deny being there
jokes.explanation: Leslie's bad lying and social awkwardness. Functional character moment.

jokes.text: STRAIGHT DOWN THE MIDDLE. NO HOOK, NO SPIN, NO FUSS. ANYTHING MORE AND THIS BECOMES FIGURE SKATING.
jokes.punchline: His dismissal of technique as unnecessarily fancy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron philosophy applied to bowling. His anti-flourish stance is very character-consistent.

jokes.text: COME ON, BIG GIRL. LET'S KNOCK THESE LITTLE PINS DOWN.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to be casual while bowling
jokes.punchline: Her awkward trash talk to the bowling ball
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's awkwardness with casual behavior. Character-appropriate cringe.

jokes.text: OH, MY GOD, ARE YOU SERIOUS?
jokes.punchline: Her shocked reaction to her own gutter ball
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy setup. Leslie's overconfidence meeting reality.

jokes.text: SON, PEOPLE CAN SEE YOU.
jokes.punchline: Ron's understated advice about public dignity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron fatherly advice. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: I'M THINKING ABOUT THE BIG PICTURE. I'M TRYING TO MAKE THE PICTURE BIGGER BY INCLUDING THIS GUY IN IT.
jokes.setup: Ben asking about the big picture
jokes.punchline: Leslie's convoluted justification for obsessing over one person
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie logic-pretzel moment. Her ability to rationalize anything.

jokes.text: I'LL JUST SAY HI TO HIM, SHAKE HIS HAND, BUY A HIM A FEW BEERS, SHARE A FEW LAUGHS, BOWL A FEW FRAMES, LOSE INTENTIONALLY TO MAKE HIM FEEL GOOD, FRIEND HIM ON FACEBOOK. AND BY THE END OF THE NIGHT, HE WILL BE MINE.
jokes.setup: Leslie promising to treat Derek like everyone else
jokes.punchline: Her elaborate manipulation plan disguised as casual interaction
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure. Perfect Leslie over-planning and the ominous 'he will be mine' ending.

jokes.text: WHOA, 'THAR' SHE BOWLS.
jokes.punchline: Her forced nautical pun attempt
jokes.explanation: Leslie's awkward attempt at being casual and funny. The forced pun shows her social awkwardness.

jokes.text: OH, NO. NO, I'M JUST-- I'M JUST BEING CAS.
jokes.setup: Derek asking if Leslie has scoliosis
jokes.punchline: Leslie's awkward abbreviation of 'casual'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. 'Being cas' perfectly captures Leslie's failed attempt at coolness.

jokes.text: REALLY? WHO KNEW? I KNEW.
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate contradiction of her own surprise
jokes.explanation: Good quick contradiction showing Leslie's need to appear knowledgeable. Nice economy.

jokes.text: THAT'S HOW I MOTIVATE MYSELF, YOU KNOW? NEVER GOOD ENOUGH.
jokes.setup: Derek asking why Leslie's mad about bowling a strike
jokes.punchline: Leslie's awkward oversharing about her psychology
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie oversharing. Shows her perfectionism and social awkwardness.

jokes.text: THESE BEERS ARE AS COLD AS THE TUKTOYAKTUK WINTER ROAD.
jokes.punchline: Her oddly specific geographic reference
jokes.explanation: Very Leslie-specific reference showing her encyclopedic knowledge used inappropriately.

jokes.text: YOU WATCH ICE ROAD TRUCKERS? YEAH, DUDE. IT'S MY GUILTY PLEASURE.
jokes.punchline: Leslie's forced casual response and trying to relate
jokes.explanation: Leslie trying to be relatable by claiming the show is a guilty pleasure when she probably studies it.

jokes.text: TOM, I'M ASKING YOU AS A MAN TO STOP THIS IMMEDIATELY.
jokes.punchline: Ron's serious appeal to Tom's masculinity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron response to Tom's excess. The formal 'as a man' appeal is very Ron.

jokes.text: WHAT THE [bleep]?
jokes.punchline: His shocked reaction to presumably bad bowling
jokes.explanation: Basic reaction humor. Tom's overconfidence meeting reality but nothing special.

jokes.text: NO, MIRA, MIRA, MIRA, MIRA, MIRA. IT'S, LIKE, WHATEVER YOU WANT. LIKE, $10. IT DON'T MATTER.
jokes.punchline: Her completely disinterested sales technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect April apathy applied to fundraising. Her complete lack of sales enthusiasm is very her.

jokes.text: [southern accent] WELL, I RECKON IT'S JUST LIKE GRAMMY MARTHA TOLD ME AND MY COUSINS, 'YOU CAN'T EAT THE BISCUITS IF YOU DON'T PAY FOR THE FLOUR.'
jokes.punchline: His bizarre fake accent and made-up folksy wisdom
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy random persona. The fake wisdom is absurd but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: PUT THE PHONE DOWN, TAKE A DEEP BREATH, AND THEN YOU ARE GOING TO TELL STEVEN THAT YOU WILL BE TREATED WITH RESPECT, OKAY?
jokes.punchline: Turning into relationship counseling
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from fundraising to therapy. Shows Ben's caring nature.

jokes.text: ALL I KNOW IS THAT I JUST WANT TO LIVE IN A WORLD THAT'S A BETTER PLACE, AND YOUR CONTRIBUTION WILL DEFINITELY HELP US GET THERE, TO THE... [sighs] BETTER-PLACE WORLD, FRED.
jokes.setup: Donna trying to be inspirational on a call
jokes.punchline: Her verbal stumble into awkward redundancy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Donna struggling with sincerity. The verbal fumble feels realistic.

jokes.text: MAN, MOVE OVER, PINS. THERE'S A NEW SHERIFF IN TOWN. YOU ARE SO DOWN WITH STRIKES, THEY SHOULD CALL YOU NORMA RAE.
jokes.punchline: Her forced pop culture reference and awkward phrasing
jokes.explanation: Leslie's awkward attempts at cool trash talk. The Norma Rae reference is very Leslie but forced.

jokes.text: WHO'S THAT? IT'S A... SALLY FIELD MOVIE ABOUT UNIONS. DOESN'T MATTER.
jokes.setup: Derek asking who Norma Rae is
jokes.punchline: Leslie's awkward over-explanation followed by dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - making an obscure reference then having to explain it awkwardly.

jokes.text: TRY NOT TO BREAK A NAIL.
jokes.punchline: His casual sexist dig
jokes.explanation: Generic sexist humor establishing Derek as a jerk. Functional but unimaginative.

jokes.text: AH, CLASSIC DEREK WITH THE ZINGS. MAN, THAT'S WHAT BOWLING IS ALL ABOUT--HEY.
jokes.punchline: Leslie's forced enthusiasm for his 'zings'
jokes.explanation: Leslie's desperate attempt to stay positive in face of obvious rudeness. Good cringe.

jokes.text: I REALIZE THAT, BUT I WILL KNOCK DOWN THIS PIN, AND THEN THIS PIN WILL KNOCK DOWN ALL THE OTHER PINS.
jokes.setup: Ben's bowling advice about not fixating on one pin
jokes.punchline: Leslie's literal interpretation as campaign strategy
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor work. Leslie missing Ben's broader point and sticking to her plan.

jokes.text: I-- I DON'T WORK HERE.
jokes.setup: Derek asking Ben to get him a beer
jokes.punchline: Ben's confused correction
jokes.explanation: Simple misunderstanding humor. Derek's casual assumption creates awkward moment.

jokes.text: CLASSIC DEREK.
jokes.setup: Derek's confusion about Ben working there
jokes.punchline: Leslie's desperate attempt to make it seem charming
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier line. Leslie's continued forced enthusiasm.

jokes.text: I AM GOING TO ASK MILLICENT GERGICH TO MOVE IN WITH ME. D-I--I MEAN, IF THAT'S OKAY WITH YOU OF COURSE, JERRY.
jokes.punchline: His awkward correction to ask Jerry's permission
jokes.explanation: Good Chris social awkwardness. His belated realization about asking the father.

jokes.text: I'VE DONE SOME READING, AND AN OCTAGON IS THE OPTIMAL SHAPE FOR A HOME IN TERMS OF ENERGY FLOW.
jokes.punchline: His pseudoscientific justification for octagonal houses
jokes.explanation: Very Chris new-age nonsense delivered with complete sincerity. Good absurdist specificity.

jokes.text: JERRY, I LOVE IT WHEN YOU PULL RANK.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking Chris to take his break in the living room
jokes.punchline: Chris's enthusiasm for Jerry's mild assertion
jokes.explanation: Chris's positive reaction to everything, even mild authority. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: MILLI'S GONNA BREAK UP WITH CHRIS.
jokes.setup: Chris's excitement about moving in together
jokes.punchline: Jerry's whispered revelation creating dramatic irony
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony setup. Creates tension and comedy from audience knowledge.

jokes.text: I AM VERY ANGRY RIGHT NOW.
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact announcement of anger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron emotional expression - completely direct and understated despite anger.

jokes.text: MY FINGER WAS IN THERE. RON CRUSHED MY FINGER. I THINK IT MIGHT BE BROKEN.
jokes.punchline: His dramatic injury report
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy setup. Tom's overdramatic reaction fits his character.

jokes.text: ARE YOU A FEMALE BIRD?
jokes.punchline: Ron's dismissive question about his complaining
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron insult. Perfect economy and originality. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: YOU DID THIS ON PURPOSE. YOU'RE JEALOUS OF MY GIFT.
jokes.punchline: His paranoid accusation about Ron's jealousy
jokes.explanation: Good Tom narcissism. His assumption that everything revolves around his talent.

jokes.text: COME ON, TWEETY BIRD, LET'S GET YOU SOME ICE.
jokes.punchline: Ron's bird reference callback while helping him
jokes.explanation: Good callback and character moment. Ron helping despite his insults shows his caring side.

jokes.text: FOR REALS, CAN I COUNT ON YOUR VOTE?
jokes.setup: Leslie asking Derek for his vote
jokes.punchline: Her forced casual language
jokes.explanation: Leslie trying to be cool with 'for reals' - shows her desperation to connect.

jokes.text: I DON'T LIKE YOU, OKAY?
jokes.setup: Leslie's confusion about not getting his vote after bowling
jokes.punchline: Derek's brutally honest response
jokes.explanation: Brutal honesty that cuts through Leslie's assumptions. Good escalation and economy.

jokes.text: I AM A REALLY GOOD BOWLER. ASK RON.
jokes.punchline: Leslie's final desperate appeal to the running gag
jokes.explanation: Good callback use in moment of desperation. Shows Leslie clinging to her defense.

jokes.text: I DON'T KNOW WHO RON IS.
jokes.punchline: Derek's complete dismissal of her go-to authority
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of the running gag. Shows the futility of Leslie's approach.

jokes.text: I'D LIKE TO INTRODUCE YOU TO MY GOOD FRIEND, ANYONE ELSE.
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic introduction emphasizing Derek's unimportance
jokes.explanation: Excellent sarcastic line. Perfect structure and very quotable. Good Ben moment.

jokes.text: I'D LIKE TO INTRODUCE DEREK'S ASS TO MY FOOT.
jokes.punchline: Leslie's aggressive response using Ben's format
jokes.explanation: Good escalation using Ben's format. Shows Leslie's breaking point.

jokes.text: $20? THANK YOU. UH, YOU KNOW, WE'RE REALLY LOOKING FOR DONATIONS MORE IN THE $10,000 RANGE.
jokes.punchline: His immediate upselling to ridiculous amount
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom greed and overreach. Good escalation from gratitude to more demands.

jokes.text: WHAT, CHAMPION? YOU NEED TO GO OUTSIDE NOW? COME ON. THAT A BOY. SORRY, HE'S-- HATES AWKWARD SITUATIONS.
jokes.punchline: Andy using his dog as excuse to escape, attributing human emotions to him
jokes.explanation: Good Andy social escape mechanism. The dog explanation is perfectly absurd and character-fitting.

jokes.text: TERRIBLE MOONWALK.
jokes.setup: Visual of someone doing a bad moonwalk
jokes.punchline: April's blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan. Excellent economy but simple observation.

jokes.text: DON'T CARE.
jokes.punchline: Andy's complete indifference to quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy response. His confidence despite criticism is very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: YEAH, WHAT'S UP NOW, HUH?
jokes.punchline: Her unsportsmanlike gloating
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie losing her composure. Basic trash talk but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: AW, YOU GOT A SPARE. THAT'S SO CUTE.
jokes.punchline: Leslie's condescending praise
jokes.explanation: Leslie's mean girl moment. Shows her competitive side breaking through.

jokes.text: I WISHED FOR HIS HAPPINESS TO GO AWAY. I MIGHT BE A WIZARD.
jokes.punchline: April's deadpan claim of magical powers
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with perfect April dark humor. The wizard claim is delightfully absurd.

jokes.text: YEAH, I'LL JUST WRITE IN 'BITCH.'
jokes.setup: Leslie asking for Derek's vote after winning
jokes.punchline: Derek's crude response
jokes.explanation: Derek revealing his true nature. Functional escalation but crude rather than clever.

jokes.text: THAT WAS AWESOME.
jokes.punchline: Leslie's enthusiastic approval of violence
jokes.explanation: Leslie's unexpected reaction to violence. Shows her protective side for Ben.

jokes.text: I HAVE A LOT OF ADRENALINE RIGHT NOW.
jokes.punchline: His explanation for weird behavior
jokes.explanation: Ben's awkward post-fight energy. Good character moment showing his inexperience with conflict.

jokes.text: DID YOU WRITE DOWN HOW AWESOME IT WAS?
jokes.setup: Police taking a report about Ben punching Derek
jokes.punchline: Leslie's suggestion for the official record
jokes.explanation: Leslie's continued enthusiasm about the punch. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: WOW, YOU AND ME, HUH? HURT 'FINGIES.'
jokes.setup: Tom and Ben both having injuries
jokes.punchline: Tom's forced bonding attempt with baby talk
jokes.explanation: Tom trying to create connection through shared injury. The baby talk is appropriately cringe.

jokes.text: NO, NO, TWO TOTALLY DIFFERENT INJURIES.
jokes.setup: Tom's attempt to bond over injuries
jokes.punchline: Ben's firm rejection of the comparison
jokes.explanation: Ben's dignity in distinguishing his heroic injury from Tom's accident. Good character moment.

jokes.text: APRIL WINS TWO TICKETS TO THE PAWNEE MONO-PLEX.
jokes.punchline: April winning despite her lack of effort
jokes.explanation: Ironic victory for April. Rewards her earlier spite-motivated effort.

jokes.text: [laughs] YES. THAT'S MY WIFE.
jokes.punchline: Andy's proud celebration of April's victory
jokes.explanation: Sweet Andy moment. His pride in April's success is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: WHOO! KING KONG AIN'T GOT NOTHING ON ME. OW, MY 'FINGIE' STILL HURTS.
jokes.punchline: His bravado immediately undermined by pain complaint
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Tom's machismo. The structure works well with the immediate contradiction.

jokes.text: OH, THE BRAVERY. THE PERSEVERANCE. YOU'RE AN AMERICAN HERO.
jokes.punchline: April's over-the-top sarcastic praise
jokes.explanation: Classic April sarcasm. Good deflation of Tom's self-importance.

jokes.text: WELL, THE HEADLINE I WOULD'VE GONE WITH IS 'NO-STRIKE-BOWLING BOWLER STRUCK BY KNOPE'S STRIKING BEAU.'
jokes.punchline: Leslie's overly complicated pun-heavy alternative
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie over-complication. The puns are forced but very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: NOT TODAY. I DON'T DESERVE IT.
jokes.setup: Ben asking if Leslie needs more whipped cream
jokes.punchline: Leslie's dramatic self-denial
jokes.explanation: Leslie's dramatic guilt about the situation. Character-appropriate overreaction.

jokes.text: BUT THIS IS RIDICULOUS. I DO NEED MORE WHIPPED CREAM. MA'AM.
jokes.setup: Leslie's dramatic denial of whipped cream
jokes.punchline: Her immediate reversal and formal request
jokes.explanation: Good quick reversal showing Leslie's inability to deny herself comfort. The formal 'ma'am' is perfect.

jokes.text: WELL, AS YOU MAY KNOW, MILLICENT GERGICH ENDED OUR RELATIONSHIP LAST NIGHT, WHICH WAS DISAPPOINTING. BUT HERE'S WHY IT MAY BE THE GREATEST THING THAT EVER HAPPENED.
jokes.punchline: His immediate pivot to extreme positivity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris response to adversity. His manic positivity in face of heartbreak is very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: YEAH, GOOD POINT.
jokes.setup: Chris's enthusiastic reframing of his breakup
jokes.punchline: April's flat, non-committal response
jokes.explanation: Perfect April non-engagement. Excellent economy in showing her discomfort with emotions.

jokes.text: THEY'RE MOVIE TICKETS, CHRIS. THEY'RE, LIKE, 8 BUCKS. IT'S A GESTURE.
jokes.setup: Chris being overly grateful for the tickets
jokes.punchline: April's blunt assessment of their low value
jokes.explanation: Good April directness cutting through Chris's emotion. Shows her discomfort with gratitude.

jokes.text: I BOUGHT ANOTHER ONE BECAUSE I THOUGHT THAT MAYBE YOU, ME, AND ANDY COULD GO TO THE MOVIES SOMETIME. JUST TAKE THE STUPID TICKETS. I'M JUST TRYING TO BE NICE.
jokes.punchline: Her awkward vulnerability followed by defensive hostility
jokes.explanation: Excellent April character moment. Her kindness masked by aggression is perfectly done.

jokes.text: AND HE CALLED ME BY MY SECOND LEAST FAVORITE TERM FOR A WOMAN
jokes.setup: Leslie describing Derek's behavior at the press conference
jokes.punchline: Her specific ranking of offensive terms
jokes.explanation: Very Leslie specific categorization. The ranking system applied to insults is character-perfect.

jokes.text: AND MY CAMPAIGN MANAGER AND I MADE OUT A LOT AFTERWARD. AH, I PROBABLY SHOULDN'T HAVE SAID THAT.
jokes.setup: Leslie defending Ben's actions at press conference
jokes.punchline: Her TMI admission and immediate regret
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie oversharing followed by self-awareness. Good structure with the immediate correction.

jokes.text: I FEEL LIKE YOU'RE BEING KIND OF A BITCH RIGHT NOW.
jokes.setup: Derek responding to Leslie's press conference
jokes.punchline: His immediate repetition of the offensive behavior
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Derek hasn't learned. Proves Leslie's point immediately.

jokes.text: IF YOU DO NOT VOTE FOR ME, MY BOYFRIEND MIGHT BEAT YOU UP.
jokes.punchline: Her mock-threatening campaign promise
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Leslie's defiance. Turning violence into campaign strategy is absurd but character-fitting.

jokes.text: YEAH, THAT GUY SAID HE DIDN'T LIKE MY EARRINGS. GO PUNCH HIM.
jokes.setup: New focus group praising Leslie's toughness
jokes.punchline: Immediately requesting violence for trivial slight
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how the violence strategy could backfire. Absurd proportional response.

jokes.text: SURE.
jokes.setup: Request to punch someone over earrings
jokes.punchline: Ben's casual agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response. Ben's transformation into willing enforcer is good character evolution.

jokes.text: SON OF A BITCH.
jokes.setup: Ron seeing something at the bowling alley
jokes.punchline: His rare display of strong emotion
jokes.explanation: Ron's reaction suggests impressive bowling. His emotional outburst is notable for him.

jokes.text: I WAS NEVER HERE, AND YOU WILL NEVER SPEAK OF THIS AGAIN.
jokes.setup: Someone wanting to put Ron's perfect game on the wall
jokes.punchline: Ron's secretive response to his bowling achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment. His desire for privacy even about achievements is completely character-consistent. Very quotable.
